assertion_results.assertion: Step 0.3 reads the Last-Updated timestamp from the security-matrix.md HTML comment and parses the ISO 8601 value
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 36-38 show the HTML comment '<!-- Last-Updated: 2026-05-01T10:00:00Z -->' and line 41 shows 'Parsed timestamp: 2026-05-01T10:00:00Z' confirming extraction and parsing of the ISO 8601 value.

assertion_results.assertion: Step 0.3 detects that the matrix is older than the 14-day default threshold and displays a staleness warning
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 44-45 show 'Age: 61 days since last update', 'Threshold: 14 days (default)', and 'Result: STALE'. Lines 59-73 display the staleness warning for both streams.

assertion_results.assertion: The staleness warning includes the stream name, the last-updated date, and the number of days since the last update
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 60 and 66 show warnings with all three elements: stream name ('stream 2.1.x' / 'stream 2.2.x'), last-updated date ('2026-05-01'), and days since update ('61 days ago').

assertion_results.assertion: The warning presents three options: refresh now (re-run setup Step 10.6), proceed anyway, or stop
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 62-64 and 68-70 present three options: 'Refresh now -- re-run matrix population (setup Step 10.6) for this stream', 'Proceed anyway -- continue triage with the current matrix', and 'Stop -- halt triage so I can investigate'.

assertion_results.assertion: Step 0.3 runs before Step 0.5 (JIRA Access Initialization) — no Jira operations are attempted before the staleness check
assertion_results.evidence: Lines 77-86 explicitly document execution order showing Step 0.3 as current (position 2) before Step 0.5 (position 3). Line 86 states: 'No Jira operations (MCP calls, REST API calls, or JQL queries) have been attempted at this point.'
